message.content:
You are rewriting AsciiDoc spec files for the Recipe Bottle project. This is Notch 3 of ₢A_AAK — the GAR categorical-layout migration. The code has already been migrated; spec files still describe the old layout and must be updated to match the new one. Your goal is **grep-zero**: after your work, no stale patterns remain in your files.

## Binding path shapes (post-migration reality, do not deviate)

```
Arks:        ${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/<hallmark>/<basename>:<hallmark>
Reliquaries: ${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/<date>/<tool>:<date>
Enshrines:   ${z_gar_base}/${RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/<anchor>:<anchor>
```

Root constants (RBGL_*) kindle from `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}<category>`. Category literals: `"hallmarks"`, `"reliquaries"`, `"enshrines"`. Ark basenames: `image`, `about`, `vouch`, `pouch`, `diags`, `attest`.

## Binding vocabulary conversions

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-image` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/image:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-about` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/about:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-vouch` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/vouch:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-pouch` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/pouch:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-diags` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/diags:<hallmark>` |
| `<vessel>:<hallmark>-attest-<ARCH>` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/attest:<hallmark>-<ARCH>` |
| `{SIGIL}:{HALLMARK}-image` or `{VESSEL}:{HALLMARK}-image` | `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/image:<hallmark>` (local kludge/graft use this shape too) |
| `vouches:<hallmark>-vouch` (aggregator) | DISSOLVED — hallmark-only lookup via subtree navigation |
| "vouches superdirectory/package/aggregator" phrase | rephrase as "hallmark subtree" or drop |

When prose describes "hyphen-suffix tag", "tagged as `-image`", etc., rewrite to match basename/subtree shape.

## Your files (4 total)

1. **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSAG-ark_graft.adoc`** — spec for graft (local push to GAR). 3 hits near lines 80, 136, 149: `{GAR_HOST}/{GAR_PATH}/{VESSEL_SIGIL}:{HALLMARK}-image` registry tag, `{VESSEL}:{HALLMARK}-image` push target, reference to `-about` artifact. Update registry tag grammar to the new shape. Note: graft has NO pouch, NO about, NO diags — only image and vouch at the local-kludge side, image goes to GAR; narrative around these absences should stay intact.

2. **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSAK-ark_kludge.adoc`** — spec for kludge (local development build). 3 hits near lines 41, 45, 58: `{GAR_BASE}/{SIGIL}:{HALLMARK}-image` tag (to match compose YAML), `docker tag` adding `-vouch` suffix, narrative about "vouch tag is a same-image alias". Update the tag grammar to new basename shape. Kludge writes images to the **local Docker image store** (not GAR in kludge mode), but the tag reference shape must still match the compose YAML interpolation which now uses `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/image:<hallmark>`.

3. **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSAP-ark_plumb.adoc`** — spec for plumb. Roughly 7 hits. Has a "hallmark-only (single argument)" mode that "resolves the vessel via the vouches superdirectory" (lines ~22, 30, 45, 47, 120–121). This vouches-superdirectory mechanism no longer exists; hallmark-only mode now navigates `<prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/` subtree directly. Also lines ~61, 69: `{VESSEL}:{HALLMARK}-about`, `-vouch` registry API extraction paths — update to new shape. Rewrite the "vouches hallmark-only mode" section to describe subtree-based lookup.

4. **`Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc`** — spec for jettison. 1 hit on line 9: NOTE referencing "three artifacts: `-image`, `-about`, `-vouch`" when describing abjure as the alternative to jettison. Update the reference to describe the hallmark subtree / basename shape. Keep the contrast between jettison (single tag delete) and abjure (full hallmark teardown) intact.

## How to work

1. Read each file first. Understand the existing prose structure and tone.
2. Edit in place using the Edit tool. Preserve the asciidoc style: attribute refs, anchors, `//axho*` section markers, bullet structure.
3. Don't add comments explaining changes. Don't add emojis.
4. For RBSAP, the hallmark-only mode section (lines ~120–121) needs substantive rewrite, not just token replacement.
5. For RBSAK, the kludge operation still works on the **local** Docker image store; update tag names but don't imply it now goes to GAR.

## When complete

Report back (under 250 words):
- Files edited, with approximate hit count cleaned per file
- Any substantive prose rewrites (esp. RBSAP hallmark-only mode)
- Any surprising findings or inconsistencies
- Any remaining concerns

**Do not use `git` commands. Do not commit. Do not run tests. Edit files only.**

content.text: I'll start by reading all four spec files to understand their structure before editing.

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_graft}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation grafts a locally-built container image into
5	{rbtgi_repository}
6	via native docker/podman push.
7	No Cloud Build involvement in the image push — the image is already built on the local workstation.
8	No crane dependency — docker tag + docker push is sufficient for locally-present images.
9	No dirty-tree guard — git state is irrelevant to an already-built container.
10	
11	NOTE: The name "graft" comes from botanical grafting — foreign material spliced onto rootstock.
12	The graft may take or may not.
13	The metaphor fits the trust posture: grafted images have no provenance chain and receive a GRAFTED verdict from
14	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}.
15	
16	NOTE: Graft uses whatever container runtime the `docker` command resolves to in PATH, matching other {rbtr_director}-side operations.
17	No separate regime variable governs runtime selection for graft.
18	
19	The
20	{rbtr_director}
21	grafts an image:
22	
23	//axhopt_typed_parameter
24	{rbtga_ark}
25	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
26	.. {rbbc_require}
27	vessel provided as first argument
28	.. {rbbc_require}
29	local image reference provided as second argument (or defaulted from {rbrv_graft_image})
30	.. {rbbc_require}
31	vessel mode is {rbrv_vessel_mode_graft}
32	
33	//axhos_step
34	. *Load Vessel Configuration*:
35	.. Source vessel configuration from `rbrv.env`
36	.. {rbbc_store}
37	`«VESSEL_SIGIL»` from {rbrv_sigil}
38	.. {rbbc_store}
39	`«VESSEL_MODE»` from {rbrv_vessel_mode}
40	.. {rbbc_require}
41	`«VESSEL_MODE»` is `graft`
42	
43	//axhos_step
44	. *Verify Local Image Exists*:
45	.. {rbbc_call}
46	`docker image inspect «LOCAL_IMAGE_REF»` (or podman equivalent)
47	.. {rbbc_require}
48	image exists in local container runtime
49	.. {rbbc_store}
50	`«LOCAL_DIGEST»` from local image inspect
51	.. {rbbc_store}
52	`«LOCAL_PLATFORMS»` from local image manifest (typically single-platform for local builds)
53	
54	//axhos_step
55	. *Authenticate as
56	{rbtr_director}*:
57	.. Execute
58	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
59	.. {rbbc_store}
60	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
61	
62	//axhos_step
63	. *Construct Hallmark*:
64	.. {rbbc_call}
65	query local container runtime for image creation timestamp via `docker image inspect` (the `Created` field — OCI Image Configuration spec, exposed at `.Created` by both docker and podman)
66	.. {rbbc_store}
67	`«IMAGE_CREATED»` as the image's creation timestamp, converted to `YYMMDDHHMMSS` format
68	.. {rbbc_store}
69	`«PUSH_TIMESTAMP»` as current UTC time (`YYMMDDHHMMSS`)
70	.. {rbbc_store}
71	`«HALLMARK»` as `g«IMAGE_CREATED»-r«PUSH_TIMESTAMP»`
72	.. T1 encodes when the image was created (a fact about the artifact); T2 encodes when it was pushed to GAR
73	.. NOTE: The `created` field may be `1970-01-01T00:00:00Z` for reproducible builds. This produces `g700101000000-r...` — a true statement about the image, not an error.
74	
75	//axhos_step
76	. *Tag and Push Image*:
77	.. {rbbc_call}
78	`docker login` to GAR registry with director credentials
79	.. {rbbc_store}
80	`«REGISTRY_TAG»` as `«GAR_HOST»/«GAR_PATH»/«VESSEL_SIGIL»:«HALLMARK»-image`
81	.. {rbbc_call}
82	`docker tag «LOCAL_IMAGE_REF» «REGISTRY_TAG»`
83	.. {rbbc_call}
84	`docker push «REGISTRY_TAG»`
85	.. {rbbc_require}
86	push succeeds
87	
88	//axhoot_typed_output
89	{rbtga_ark_image}
90	
91	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
92	{rbf_fact_hallmark}
93	The full
94	{rbtga_hallmark}
95	string.
96	
97	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
98	{rbf_fact_build_id}
99	Empty for graft — no
100	{gcb_build}
101	involvement in image push.
102	Written for uniform fact coverage.
103	
104	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
105	{rbf_fact_gar_root}
106	{gar_service}
107	registry prefix.
108	Composable with
109	{rbf_fact_ark_stem}
110	for full artifact references.
111	
112	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
113	{rbf_fact_ark_stem}
114	{rbrv_sigil}
115	and
116	{rbtga_hallmark}
117	joined as `«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»`.
118	Composable with suffix for artifact tags.
119	
120	//axhoot_typed_output axd_fact
121	{rbf_fact_ark_yield}
122	Single-platform
123	{rbtga_ark_image}
124	reference.
125	One fact file (graft is single-platform only).
126	
127	. *Display Results*:
128	.. {rbbc_show}
129	graft complete with hallmark tag
130	.. {rbbc_show}
131	local image reference, registry tag, digest
132	
133	//axhoc_completion
134	Successful execution pushes the locally-built image to
135	{rbtgi_repository}
136	tagged as `«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»-image`.
137	The graft operation returns `«HALLMARK»` to {rbtgo_ark_ordain}, which chains the subsequent pipeline stages automatically:
138	{rbtgo_ark_about} and {rbtgo_ark_vouch} in a single combined `builds.create` job (about steps followed by vouch steps) to assemble metadata and record the GRAFTED verdict.
139	+
140	*No SLSA provenance*: Grafted images have no Cloud Build provenance.
141	The {rbtgo_ark_vouch} pipeline records a GRAFTED verdict, explicitly marking the absence of a provenance chain.
142	This is a deliberate trust boundary — grafted images are accepted into the system but carry less attestation than conjured images.
143	+
144	*Push atomicity*: `docker push` is atomic per manifest — a failed push (network timeout, authentication expiry) leaves no partial state in GAR.
145	The director can safely retry without cleanup.
146	+
147	*Single-platform only*: Graft is specified for single-platform images only.
148	`docker push` of a locally-present image pushes a single manifest.
149	The about pipeline's platform discovery step handles this correctly, producing a single-platform `-about` artifact.
150	Multi-platform graft is not currently specified — `buildx --load` only loads one platform into the local daemon, so true multi-platform local images would require a different push mechanism.
151

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_kludge}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation builds a vessel image locally on the workstation using `docker build`, bypassing Cloud Build and GAR entirely. It produces a
5	{rbtga_hallmark}
6	with a `k` prefix (kludge) that composes cleanly with the nameplate and compose infrastructure but is obviously not a production hallmark.
7	
8	NOTE: Kludge is a deliberate development shortcut for fast edit-build-test cycles. It enables iterating on vessel Dockerfiles, entrypoint scripts, and package lists without waiting for a Cloud Build round-trip. The ugly name is intentional -- this is not part of the production hallmark pipeline.
9	
10	NOTE: The kludge hallmark format is `k«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-«git-describe»`, paralleling conjure's `c«YYMMDDHHMMSS»` and bind's `b«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-r«YYMMDDHHMMSS»`. The timestamp provides chronological sorting; the `git describe --always` suffix ties the image to a specific commit. The dirty-tree guard ensures the describe always corresponds to a committed state. The `k` prefix makes kludge hallmarks visually distinct in nameplate files and `docker image ls` output.
11	
12	The kludge operation:
13	
14	//axhopt_typed_parameter
15	{rbtga_ark}
16	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
17	.. {rbbc_require}
18	vessel provided as first argument (sigil or path)
19	.. {rbbc_require}
20	vessel is conjure mode (bind and graft vessels have no local Dockerfile)
21	.. {rbbc_require}
22	vessel has conjuring configuration (RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE, RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT)
23	
24	//axhos_step
25	. *Resolve Base Images*:
26	.. For each indexed slot (n = 1, 2, 3) where
27	{rbrv_image_origin}
28	is set, use the origin directly as the `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-arg
29	.. No enshrine anchor resolution -- kludge pulls base images from upstream registries
30	
31	//axhos_step
32	. *Assign Kludge Hallmark*:
33	.. Mint hallmark as `k«YYMMDDHHMMSS»-«git-describe»` from dispatch timestamp and `BURD_GIT_CONTEXT`
34	.. {rbbc_store}
35	`«HALLMARK»`
36	
37	//axhos_step
38	. *Build Image Locally*:
39	.. Run `docker build` with resolved `RBF_IMAGE_n` build-args
40	.. Build for host platform only (no multi-arch -- development builds run where they are built)
41	.. Tag as `«GAR_BASE»/«SIGIL»:«HALLMARK»-image` to match compose YAML interpolation format
42	
43	//axhos_step
44	. *Create Fake Vouch Tag*:
45	.. `docker tag` the image ref to also carry the `-vouch` suffix
46	.. This is the same image aliased -- no provenance verification, no DSSE envelope
47	.. Satisfies the `rbob_charge` vouch gate (`docker image inspect` on vouch ref)
48	
49	//axhos_step
50	. *Report Hallmark*:
51	.. Print the hallmark value for the operator to paste into the nameplate `.env` file
52	.. Persist hallmark to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` for downstream chaining
53	
54	== Limitations
55	
56	* Requires GCP depot configuration (foundry kindle validates `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE` exists, though kludge does not use credentials)
57	* Host platform only -- a kludge-built `linux/arm64` image on Apple Silicon will not run on `linux/amd64` hosts
58	* No about artifact, no provenance chain -- the vouch tag is a same-image alias with no attestation content
59	* Not suitable for release or delivery -- use {rbtgo_ark_conjure} for production hallmarks
60	
61	== Tabtarget
62	
63	`tt/rbw-fk.LocalKludge.sh «vessel-sigil»`
64	
65	== See Also
66	
67	* {rbtgo_ark_conjure} -- production image build via Cloud Build
68	* {rbtgo_ark_vouch} -- provenance attestation (kludge bypasses this entirely)
69	* {rbtgo_ark_summon} -- pull from GAR (not needed for kludge -- image is already local)
70

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_inspect}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation plumbs an
5	{rbtga_ark}
6	by querying GAR directly via the Docker Registry API.
7	Artifacts are fetched from the
8	{rbtga_ark_about}
9	and
10	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
11	packages in the registry.
12	No local container runtime is required.
13	Authenticates as
14	{rbtr_retriever}.
15	
16	NOTE: Two entry points provide different detail levels:
17	{rbtc_plumb_full} for complete diagnostic output and
18	{rbtc_plumb_compact} for a trust posture summary.
19	
20	NOTE: Two invocation modes:
21	Vessel+hallmark (both arguments) fetches directly from the vessel package.
22	Hallmark-only (single argument) resolves the vessel via the vouches superdirectory.
23	
24	The plumb operation:
25	
26	//axhopt_typed_parameter
27	{rbtga_ark}
28	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
29	.. If two arguments: vessel and hallmark
30	.. If one argument: hallmark only (vessel resolved via vouches lookup)
31	
32	//axhos_step
33	. *Authenticate as
34	{rbtr_retriever}*:
35	.. {rbbc_require}
36	Retriever credential file present (`RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE`)
37	.. Execute
38	{rbtoe_jwt_oauth_exchange}
39	.. {rbbc_store}
40	`«RETRIEVER_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
41	
42	//axhos_step
43	. *Resolve Vessel (hallmark-only mode)*:
44	.. {rbbc_call}
45	Registry API: extract vouch artifact from `vouches:«HALLMARK»-vouch`
46	.. {rbbc_require}
47	hallmark exists in vouches superdirectory
48	.. {rbbc_store}
49	vessel name from `vouch_summary.json` `.vessel` field
50	.. {rbbc_store}
51	vouch artifact contents for display
52	
53	//axhos_step
54	. *Load Vessel Configuration*:
55	.. {rbbc_store}
56	vessel mode (conjure, bind, or graft) from vessel regime
57	
58	//axhos_step
59	. *Fetch About Artifact from GAR*:
60	.. {rbbc_call}
61	Registry API: extract from `«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»-about`
62	.. Resolve multi-platform manifest to single platform (content is architecture-independent)
63	.. Fetch layer blob and extract: `build_info.json`, `sbom.json`, `recipe.txt`, `buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json`
64	.. Graceful degradation: absence of optional files does not affect output
65	
66	//axhos_step
67	. *Fetch Vouch Artifact from GAR* (if not already from vouches):
68	.. {rbbc_call}
69	Registry API: extract from `«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»-vouch`
70	.. Extract `vouch_summary.json` and per-platform verification files
71	
72	//axhos_step
73	. *Display Mode-Aware Sections* (both detail levels):
74	.. *Vessel Type*: mode (conjure/bind/graft), platform, build strategy (native/QEMU for conjure), moniker
75	.. *Source*: git repository, branch, commit
76	.. Branch on vessel mode:
77	+
78	**Conjure sections**:
79	.. *Builder*: Cloud Build job ID, timestamps, image URI
80	.. *SLSA Provenance*: build level, invocation ID, builder ID, predicate types, explicit boundary statements (what SLSA does and does not attest)
81	.. *Base Image*: Dockerfile FROM line, syft-detected distro
82	+
83	**Bind sections**:
84	.. *Bind Source*: original upstream image reference, digest-pin status
85	.. *Trust Boundary*: digest-pin only, no SLSA provenance, no build transcript
86	+
87	**Graft sections**:
88	.. *Graft Source*: local image reference at push time
89	.. *Trust Boundary*: no provenance chain, no build transcript — GRAFTED acknowledgment
90	+
91	**Shared sections** (all modes):
92	.. *SBOM Summary*: package count and type breakdown from syft scan
93	.. *Vouch Results*: verdict type and details:
94	... Conjure: verifier identity, per-platform verification verdicts (`SLSA_VERIFIED`)
95	... Bind: digest-pin confirmation (`DIGEST_PIN_VERIFIED`)
96	... Graft: GRAFTED acknowledgment (`GRAFTED`)
97	
98	//axhos_step
99	. *Display Full-Only Sections* ({rbtc_plumb_full} only):
100	.. *Package Inventory*: every syft-detected package (type, name, version) sorted by ecosystem
101	.. *Package Licensing*: per-package license and Package URL for compliance review
102	.. *Recipe*: the Dockerfile content from `recipe.txt` in the about artifact (available for any mode when the Dockerfile path was configured in the vessel regime). When `recipe.txt` is absent, display the Dockerfile path from `build_info.json` if available, or omit the section.
103	
104	//axhoot_typed_output
105	{rbtga_ark}
106	. *Display Results*:
107	.. {rbbc_show}
108	trust posture for the specified hallmark
109	
110	//axhoc_completion
111	Plumb is a read-only operation that queries GAR via the Docker Registry API.
112	It does not modify artifacts or require a local container runtime.
113	Authenticates as
114	{rbtr_retriever}
115	using OAuth credentials.
116	+
117	*GAR-direct extraction*: Artifacts are fetched via Registry API manifest and blob endpoints.
118	Multi-platform manifest lists are resolved to a single platform (first entry) since artifact content (JSON metadata) is architecture-independent.
119	+
120	*vouches hallmark-only mode*: When invoked with only a hallmark, plumb resolves the vessel by looking up the hallmark in the vouches superdirectory package.
121	The vouches package aggregates all vouch artifacts across all vessels, enabling reverse mapping from hallmark to vessel.
122	+
123	*Mode-aware display*: The plumb output adapts to vessel mode.
124	Bind and graft vessels display clear trust boundary statements rather than attempting to show provenance data that does not exist.
125

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_image_delete}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation jettisons a container image from
5	{rbtgi_repository}
6	using the Docker Registry HTTP API V2 implemented by
7	{gar_service}.
8	
9	NOTE: To delete an entire {rbtga_ark} (all three artifacts: `-image`, `-about`, `-vouch`), use {rbtgo_ark_abjure} instead.
10	
11	The
12	{rbtr_director}
13	jettisons an image by {rbst_locator}:
14	
15	//axhopt_typed_parameter
16	{rbst_locator}
17	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
18	.. {rbbc_require}
19	`«INPUT_LOCATOR»` provided as first argument in `moniker:tag` format
20	.. {rbbc_fatal}
21	if missing: "Locator parameter required (moniker:tag)"
22	.. {rbbc_fatal}
23	if malformed (no colon): "Invalid locator format. Expected moniker:tag"
24	.. {rbbc_store}
25	`«MONIKER»` as portion before first colon
26	.. {rbbc_store}
27	`«TAG»` as portion after first colon
28	
29	//axhos_step
30	. *Authenticate as
31	{rbtr_director}*:
32	.. Execute
33	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
34	.. {rbbc_store}
35	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
36	
37	//axhos_step
38	. *Confirm Jettison*:
39	.. Unless `--force` flag is provided:
40	... {rbbc_show}
41	`Will jettison: «INPUT_LOCATOR»`
42	... {rbbc_require}
43	user types `yes` to confirm
44	... {rbbc_fatal}
45	if not confirmed: "prompt not confirmed."
46	
47	//axhos_step
48	. *Jettison Tag*:
49	.. {rbbc_call}
50	link:https://github.com/opencontainers/distribution-spec/blob/main/spec.md#deleting-tags[Docker Registry API: DELETE manifests] with:
51	... method: `DELETE`
52	... endpoint: `«REGISTRY_BASE»/«MONIKER»/manifests/«TAG»`
53	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
54	.. {rbbc_require}
55	HTTP 202 (Accepted) or 204 (No Content) response
56	.. {rbbc_fatal}
57	if jettison fails: "Jettison failed with HTTP «STATUS_CODE»"
58	
59	//axhoot_typed_output
60	{rbst_locator}
61	. *Display Results*:
62	.. {rbbc_show}
63	`Jettisoned or nonexistent: «INPUT_LOCATOR»`
64	
65	//axhoc_completion
66	Procedure completes successfully when the tag has been jettisoned from the registry and the jettisoned message is displayed.
67

content.text:

NOTE: Two invocation modes:

input.new_string:
NOTE: Two invocation modes:
Vessel+hallmark (both arguments) fetches directly from the hallmark subtree with the vessel identity already in hand.
Hallmark-only (single argument) navigates the `hallmarks/«HALLMARK»/` subtree to locate the basenames and reads the vessel identity from the vouch artifact's `vouch_summary.json`.

content.text: Now the substantive rewrite at lines 120-121 (vouches hallmark-only mode paragraph):

input.old_string:
*vouches hallmark-only mode*: When invoked with only a hallmark, plumb resolves the vessel by looking up the hallmark in the vouches superdirectory package.
The vouches package aggregates all vouch artifacts across all vessels, enabling reverse mapping from hallmark to vessel.
input.new_string:
*Hallmark-only mode*: When invoked with only a hallmark, plumb navigates directly to the `hallmarks/«HALLMARK»/` subtree in GAR.
The categorical layout makes the hallmark itself the primary index — all basenames (`image`, `about`, `vouch`, `pouch`, `diags`, `attest`) for a single build sit together under one subtree, so no cross-vessel aggregator is needed.
The vessel identity is recovered by reading the `.vessel` field out of the subtree's `vouch` artifact, and the remaining basenames are fetched from the same subtree.

input.old_string: NOTE: To delete an entire {rbtga_ark} (all three artifacts: `-image`, `-about`, `-vouch`), use {rbtgo_ark_abjure} instead.
input.new_string: NOTE: To delete an entire {rbtga_ark} (the full `hallmarks/«HALLMARK»/` subtree — every basename including `image`, `about`, `vouch`, and any `pouch`/`diags`/`attest` tags present), use {rbtgo_ark_abjure} instead.

All four files cleaned. Summary:

**Files edited (all 4 grep-zero):**

- `RBSAG-ark_graft.adoc` — 4 edits: registry tag grammar (L80), completion prose (L136), about-artifact phrasing (L149), plus `ark_stem` fact description which embedded `«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»` (not called out in brief but stale; rewrote to describe the hallmark subtree prefix).
- `RBSAK-ark_kludge.adoc` — 3 edits: compose-YAML tag at L41, fake-vouch tag block at L45 (now names the `vouch` basename under the same hallmark subtree), limitations bullet at L58 (`-about`/`-vouch` → basename wording). Kludge is still clearly local-only; the tag shape matches the new compose interpolation without implying GAR travel.
- `RBSAP-ark_plumb.adoc` — 5 edits + 1 substantive rewrite: intro NOTE for two-mode invocation, validate-parameters step, resolve-vessel step, about/vouch fetch paths, and the completion paragraph previously titled "vouches hallmark-only mode". Rewrote that paragraph to describe direct navigation of `hallmarks/«HALLMARK»/` as a subtree where all basenames colocate, with the vessel identity recovered from `vouch_summary.json.vessel` — the aggregator model is explicitly named as unneeded rather than silently deleted, so a reader migrating from prior versions sees the shift.
- `RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc` — 1 edit: the abjure-contrast NOTE now names the full `hallmarks/«HALLMARK»/` subtree and enumerates the basenames (including the newer `pouch`/`diags`/`attest`), preserving the single-tag-vs-full-teardown contrast.

**Findings/concerns:**
- RBSAG's `ark_stem` fact was an additional stale hit beyond the three in the brief — the old "joined as `«VESSEL»:«HALLMARK»`" composition model no longer matches the new grammar where the hallmark lives twice (once as subtree segment, once as tag). Rewrote in the new shape.
- Verified no remaining hits for `vouches:`, `-image`/`-about`/`-vouch` tag suffixes, or "superdirectory"/"aggregator" (except the one deliberate disavowal line in RBSAP).
